businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and custom chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, while Q4 gross margin hit a two-year high of 20.1%. On March 21, 2026, Tesla officially launched its Terafab in-house semiconductor operation, with Cybercab mass production slated to begin in April.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy generation and storage (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (~23x), auto sector avg (~16x), and BYD (~28x). Still elevated despite sustained selloff

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus of $2.53. S&P 500 forward P/E (~20x), Rivian (unprofitable), Lucid (unprofitable). Growth premium priced in

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit a two-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Modest YoY decline, but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash supports $20B+ annual capex cycle

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed margins from price competition and expanded R&D/capex for Terafab, Cybercab

growthDrivers.title: Terafab In-House Semiconductor Operation Officially Launched Today (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla officially launched its Terafab project today, targeting production of hundreds of billions of custom chips annually. Musk warned chip supply will be insufficient within 3-4 years and is hiring semiconductor fab construction managers. 2026 capex exceeds $20B.

growthDrivers.title: $2.9B Chinese Solar Equipment Deal: 100GW US Solar Manufacturing Target
growthDrivers.description: Per Reuters exclusive, Tesla is negotiating $2.9B in solar manufacturing equipment purchases from Suzhou Maxwell Technologies and other Chinese firms. Target: 100GW US solar production by end of 2028, with Texas delivery before fall 2026.

growthDrivers.title: FSD Europe Launch: Netherlands Approval Delayed to April 10
growthDrivers.description: Netherlands FSD approval, originally expected March 20, slipped to April 10. This first European approval is critical for continent-wide FSD rollout, but the delay weakens near-term momentum alongside the NHTSA probe.

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Contract Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at its AGM that Tesla AI6 (HW6) chips will be produced at the Taylor, Texas fab using 2nm (SF2P) process. $16.5B through 2033. MPW production delayed ~6 months due to yield issues.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April Production Start, Macrohard AI Platform
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds of units per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI joint venture) is an enterprise AI platform diversifying revenue beyond vehicles.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. 8.2B miles of FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form the core moat. Samsung AI6 foundry deal and Terafab launch accelerate in-house chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles driven. Overwhelmingly more training data than any competitor.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab + Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, and Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2025: 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging tech

recentNews.headline: Terafab In-House Semiconductor Operation Goes Live; Musk Warns of Chip Shortage in 3-4 Years
recentNews.significance: Tesla officially launched Terafab today, hiring fab construction managers and committing $20B+ in 2026 capex. First automaker to build an in-house chip manufacturing operation at scale.

recentNews.headline: FSD Europe Approval (Netherlands) Delayed to April 10
recentNews.significance: Netherlands FSD approval, originally targeted for March 20, pushed back to April 10. First European market approval is pivotal for continent-wide rollout.

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Deploys Additional Warships to Middle East; Oil Prices Surge, Markets Sell Off
recentNews.significance: Pentagon dispatched 3 warships and thousands of Marines to the Middle East. Oil surged, pushing S&P 500 into its longest weekly losing streak in a year. Growth stocks hammered.

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Escalates FSD Probe to Engineering Analysis: 3.2M Vehicles, Potential Recall
recentNews.significance: NHTSA elevated its FSD investigation to Engineering Analysis, the final stage before a recall order. 9 crashes (1 fatality) linked to camera-only visibility issues. OTA fix possible.

recentNews.headline: Reuters Exclusive: Tesla in Talks to Buy $2.9B in Solar Equipment from Chinese Firms
recentNews.significance: Tesla negotiating with Suzhou Maxwell Technologies and others for solar panel and cell manufacturing equipment. Target: 100GW US solar production by 2028.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 43-analyst consensus at $407.63 (Hold). Wedbush at $600 (Outperform), BofA at $460 (Buy), BNP Paribas at $280 (Underperform), low of $19.05. 15 Strong Buy, 17 Hold, 9 Strong Sell. 10.8% upside from current price.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Engineering Analysis: One Step From Recall
risks.description: Investigation covering 3.2M vehicles escalated to final pre-recall stage. Camera-only visibility issues linked to 9 crashes (1 fatality). OTA fix possible, but hardware recall would deal a major blow to robotaxi strategy.

risks.title: Middle East Geopolitical Escalation and Oil Price Surge
risks.description: Pentagon's additional Middle East deployment sustains oil price surge. High rates plus high oil is the worst combination for growth stock valuations. S&P 500 in longest weekly losing streak.

risks.title: FSD Europe Approval Delayed (April 10)
risks.description: Netherlands FSD approval pushed from March 20 to April 10. Combined with NHTSA probe, regulatory risk is pressuring from both sides of the Atlantic.

risks.title: Fed Rate Hold, 2026 Cut Expectations Vanishing
risks.description: Markets are pricing out any 2026 rate cuts entirely. The worst rate environment for a stock trading at 220x P/E.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M EVs vs Tesla's 1.64M in 2025. 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging tech narrowing the gap.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at 220x P/E
risks.description: Versus S&P 500 avg of 23x, auto sector 16x, BYD 28x. Multiple compression risk intensifying with macro headwinds.

buyReasons.title: Terafab Launched Today: First Automaker With In-House Chip Manufacturing
buyReasons.rationale: No other automaker can match Tesla's move into in-house chip production. Musk's preemptive investment against a 3-4 year chip shortage. If successful, completes the vertical integration of AI, FSD, and Optimus.

buyReasons.title: $2.9B Solar + $16.5B Samsung AI6: Massive Capex Cycle
buyReasons.rationale: 100GW solar, in-house chip production, and Cybercab lines. Over $20B in 2026 investment could convert to revenue in 2027-2028.

buyReasons.title: RSI at 30 (Oversold), Down 26% From 52-Week High
buyReasons.rationale: RSI entering oversold territory. Historically a bounce zone. Trading below SMA-20 ($401), SMA-50 ($417), and SMA-200 ($394), suggesting technical rebound potential.

buyReasons.title: Analyst Target $407.63, 10.8% Upside
buyReasons.rationale: 43-analyst consensus implies 10.8% upside. High target of $600 represents 63% upside. Risk/reward improving at oversold levels.

overallOpinion: Tesla officially launched Terafab today, its in-house semiconductor operation. Musk warns chip supply will fall short in 3-4 years, committing $20B+ in 2026 capex.

FSD Europe's first approval (Netherlands) slipped to April 10, while NHTSA's probe has escalated to Engineering Analysis, the last stage before a recall order covering 3.2M vehicles. An OTA fix remains possible.
The 43-analyst consensus target of $408 implies 10.8% upside from $368. RSI sits at 30 (oversold), with shares trading below the 20-day ($401), 50-day ($417), and 200-day ($394) moving averages.
Hold. Catalysts are abundant but Middle East escalation and rate headwinds persist. Staged buying after Q1 earnings and FSD probe resolution is the safer approach.

investmentVerdict.summary: Terafab's launch marks the dawn of Tesla's in-house chip era, but FSD faces regulatory pressure on two fronts: Europe's approval delay (April 10) and NHTSA's escalated probe. At $367.96 with RSI at 30, the stock is technically oversold with 10.8% upside to the $408 consensus. However, Middle East geopolitical risk and vanishing rate-cut expectations create persistent headwinds. The prudent strategy is staged accumulation after Q1 earnings clarity and FSD regulatory resolution.

threads.hook: Tesla launches Terafab, but FSD is under siege
threads.text:
$TSLA $367.96 (-0.83%)

Tesla officially launched Terafab today, becoming the first automaker to build in-house chip manufacturing at scale. Musk warns chips will run short in 3-4 years. 2026 capex exceeds $20B.

But FSD is under siege. Europe's first approval (Netherlands) slipped to April 10. NHTSA escalated its probe to Engineering Analysis, covering 3.2M vehicles, one step from a recall.

RSI at 30, below all major moving averages. Catalysts are there but regulatory and macro headwinds are fierce. Hold. Target $408 (+10.8%).

Making chips is easy. Getting permission to use them is hard.

empathy.hook: Tesla at $368. A 26% drop is genuinely painful
empathy.text:
$TSLA $367.96 (-0.83%)

52-week high was $499. Now it's $368. FSD delayed in Europe, recall risk in the US. Middle East escalation and frozen rates. Being worried is completely rational.

But Terafab launched today and Cybercab production starts in April. RSI at 30 has historically marked turning points.

Selling is too late. Buying is too early. Hold. Wait for Q1 and FSD clarity. Target $408 (+10.8%).
